verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 is a secreted epididymal protein that binds to the sperm surface during epididymal transit, becoming stably associated with the sperm plasma membrane (postacrosomal/equatorial segment) where it participates in sperm-egg fusion. This stable, wash-resistant surface association on mature sperm represents a documented outer-leaflet pool, but it is restricted to the germline/gamete context. This maps to contextual/tissue_restricted_surface: the protein reaches the outer leaflet only on mature sperm, a narrow reproductive-lineage cell type.
